original_description: Multi-perspective code review using an RLM swarm -- spawns specialised reviewer sub-queries (security, performance, correctness, API design) via capability-routed rlm_query calls, then reconciles findings into a single ranked report with P0/P1/P2 severity and a confidence score. Use when the change spans many files (rough threshold: more than five files or five hundred lines), touches security-critical code, or the user explicitly asks for an "adversarial", "swarm", or "multi-perspective" review. Prefer `code-review` for routine single-file review; prefer `structural-pr-review` for PR-shaped review with diagrams; prefer `quality-gate` for full pre-merge V-model evidence.
best_description: Multi-perspective code review using an RLM swarm -- spawns specialised reviewer sub-queries (security, performance, correctness, API design) via capability-routed rlm_query calls, then reconciles findings into a single ranked report with P0/P1/P2 severity and a confidence score. Use when the change spans many files (rough threshold: more than five files or five hundred lines), touches security-critical code, or the user explicitly asks for an "adversarial", "swarm", or "multi-perspective" review. Prefer `code-review` for routine single-file review; prefer `structural-pr-review` for PR-shaped review with diagrams; prefer `quality-gate` for full pre-merge V-model evidence.
final_description: Use this skill when the user wants a heavy, multi-reviewer code review on a substantial change -- phrases like "adversarial review", "swarm review", "multi-perspective", "multi-role", "multi-reviewer", "reconciled review", "deterministic multi-reviewer", or asking for several reviewer roles (security + correctness + performance + API design, optionally plus concurrency or schema) with conflicts surfaced, P0/P1/P2 severity ranking, and a confidence score. Also trigger for large PRs (roughly more than 5 files or 500 lines, e.g. 12-25 file migrations, refactors, or auth/database/schema changes) or security-critical subsystems (auth, JWT, sessions, crypto, schema migrations) where one review pass would miss conflicts between concerns. Trigger on mentions of "capability-routed", "rlm_query reviewers", or RLM swarm reviewers. Prefer `code-review` for single-file reviews, `structural-pr-review` for PR comments with diagrams, and `quality-gate` for full pre-merge V-model evidence.

history.description: Multi-perspective code review using an RLM swarm -- spawns specialised reviewer sub-queries (security, performance, correctness, API design) via capability-routed rlm_query calls, then reconciles findings into a single ranked report with P0/P1/P2 severity and a confidence score. Use when the change spans many files (rough threshold: more than five files or five hundred lines), touches security-critical code, or the user explicitly asks for an "adversarial", "swarm", or "multi-perspective" review. Prefer `code-review` for routine single-file review; prefer `structural-pr-review` for PR-shaped review with diagrams; prefer `quality-gate` for full pre-merge V-model evidence.

history.description: Use this skill for multi-perspective ("swarm", "adversarial", "multi-role", "multi-reviewer") code reviews that spawn specialised reviewers (security, correctness, performance, API design, plus optional roles like concurrency or schema) and reconcile findings into one ranked report with P0/P1/P2 severity and a confidence score. Trigger when: the user asks for adversarial, swarm, multi-perspective, multi-role, or reconciled review; the change is large (roughly >5 files or >500 lines, e.g. 12-file migration, 18-file auth PR, 25-file refactor); or touches security-critical subsystems (auth, crypto, sessions, schema migrations) where one pass would miss conflicts between concerns. Also trigger on "capability-routed", "rlm_query reviewers", or "P0/P1/P2 ranking with confidence". Prefer `code-review` for single-file review, `structural-pr-review` for PR comments with diagrams, `quality-gate` for full pre-merge V-model evidence.
